title: History Close-Up
description: Narrative nonfiction for curious readers: real people, real turning points, and the choices that changed the world.

stories.title: Napoleon: The Corsican Who Reshaped Europe
stories.description: From the mountains of Corsica to the battlefields of Europe, Napoleon Bonaparte rose faster than almost anyone in history. This factual account follows his conquests, his reforms, his disasters, and the long shadow he left behind.

stories.title: Lawrence Washington and the War Before the Revolution
stories.description: Before George Washington led a revolution, his older half-brother Lawrence marched into Britain's imperial wars. His short life linked George to the world of ships, empire, and Mount Vernon.

stories.title: Henry Knox and the Winter Guns
stories.description: An artillery story built from sledges, frozen roads, and stubborn determination. Henry Knox's hard winter journey from Ticonderoga helped force the British out of Boston.

stories.title: Lafayette Between Two Revolutions
stories.description: The Marquis de Lafayette was still a teenager when he sailed to America. This factual story follows him from Washington's camp to Yorktown and into the storms of the French Revolution.

stories.title: James Lafayette: Double Agent at Yorktown
stories.description: Born into slavery in Virginia, James Lafayette helped deceive the British during the final great campaign of the Revolutionary War. This factual account follows his intelligence work, Yorktown, and the harder struggle that came after victory: winning his own freedom.

stories.title: George Washington and the Weight of Command
stories.description: From Virginia surveyor to commander in chief and first president, this factual story follows George Washington with special attention to the moments when he chose restraint instead of personal rule.

stories.title: Nathanael Greene and the War of Exhaustion
stories.description: Nathanael Greene inherited a broken southern army and used movement, patience, and pressure to wear down Cornwallis. This factual account follows Cowpens, the Race to the Dan, Guilford Courthouse, and the road toward Yorktown.

stories.title: Julius Caesar and the Fall of the Republic
stories.description: This factual narrative follows Caesar from Roman politics to Gaul, civil war, dictatorship, and assassination, showing why his life still shapes every story about power and republics in crisis.

stories.title: Abigail Adams and the Revolution at Home
stories.description: Abigail Adams did not command armies, but she watched a revolution unfold from farm, household, and letter desk. This factual narrative follows her through wartime Massachusetts and the political intelligence preserved in her letters.

stories.title: John Paul Jones and the War at Sea
stories.description: John Paul Jones carried the American Revolution onto the ocean with raids, stubborn nerve, and one of the most famous naval battles in history. This factual story shows why sea power mattered to the Revolution.

stories.title: Baron von Steuben and the Army at Valley Forge
stories.description: Friedrich Wilhelm von Steuben brought European military method to a hungry American army and helped turn Valley Forge into a training ground instead of a graveyard.

stories.title: Deborah Sampson: The Hidden Soldier
stories.description: Deborah Sampson enlisted as Robert Shurtliff, served in the Continental Army, and later forced the new nation to recognize what she had done.

stories.title: Phillis Wheatley and the Words of Liberty
stories.description: Phillis Wheatley was brought to Boston in bondage and became one of the most remarkable poets of the Revolutionary era. This factual story follows her writing, her public fame, and the sharp limits the new nation placed around freedom.

stories.title: Benedict Arnold and the Price of Treason
stories.description: Benedict Arnold began the Revolution as one of the American cause's boldest officers and ended it as its most famous traitor. This factual narrative follows his rise, his bitterness, and the failed plot that destroyed his name.

stories.title: Francis Marion and the Swamp War
stories.description: Francis Marion became famous as the "Swamp Fox," but the real story is sharper than the legend. This factual narrative follows his South Carolina background, his partisan war against British control, and the limits of memory in a brutal civil war.

stories.title: Mercy Otis Warren and the Pen of Revolution
stories.description: Mercy Otis Warren fought the Revolution with plays, letters, arguments, and history. This factual narrative follows how one of the founding era's sharpest political writers turned private observation into public force.

stories.title: Rochambeau and the Allied Road to Yorktown
stories.description: Rochambeau did not come to America to steal the spotlight from George Washington. He came to help win a war. This factual narrative follows the French general, the alliance that changed the Revolution, and the careful march that ended at Yorktown.

stories.title: Benjamin Tallmadge and the Culper Ring
stories.description: Benjamin Tallmadge helped build the Continental Army's most effective spy network. This factual narrative follows the hidden war of codes, boats, couriers, and secrets around British-occupied New York.

stories.title: Tadeusz Kosciuszko and the River Forts
stories.description: Tadeusz Kosciuszko came to America with engineering skill and a serious moral vision. This factual narrative follows his work at Saratoga and West Point, where earth, timber, and geometry helped shape the Revolution.

stories.title: Daniel Morgan and the Trap at Cowpens
stories.description: Daniel Morgan rose from rough frontier labor to become one of the Revolution's sharpest battlefield thinkers. This factual narrative follows his riflemen, Saratoga, and the clever plan that broke Tarleton at Cowpens.

stories.title: Thomas Paine and the Words That Marched
stories.description: Thomas Paine reached America just before the Revolution and quickly became one of its loudest and clearest voices. This factual narrative follows Common Sense, the Crisis papers, and the writer who helped move a public toward independence.

stories.title: Crispus Attucks and the Night on King Street
stories.description: Crispus Attucks died in the Boston Massacre years before independence was declared. This factual narrative follows the uncertain details of his life, the violence on King Street, and the way one death entered revolutionary memory.

stories.title: Samuel Adams and the Fire in Boston
stories.description: Samuel Adams did not command armies, but he helped turn Boston's anger into organized resistance. This factual narrative follows the political work that pushed protest toward independence.

stories.title: Battle of Britain
stories.description: A fact-based account of the air campaign that decided whether Britain could resist invasion in 1940.

stories.title: Dunkirk Evacuation
stories.description: A factual narrative of Operation Dynamo, when hundreds of thousands of Allied troops were pulled off the beaches of France under fire.

stories.title: D-Day Landings
stories.description: This factual story follows the opening day of the Normandy invasion, from airborne drops before dawn to the hard fight for the beaches.

stories.title: Battle of Stalingrad
stories.description: A factual account of the brutal battle on the Volga that trapped a German army and changed the course of the Eastern Front.

stories.title: Fall of Constantinople
stories.description: This factual narrative follows the 1453 siege that ended the Byzantine Empire and remade the balance of power in the eastern Mediterranean.

stories.title: Spanish Armada
stories.description: A factual retelling of the 1588 campaign in which England faced Spain's great invasion fleet in the Channel and beyond.

stories.title: Gunpowder Plot
stories.description: This factual account follows the 1605 conspiracy to blow up king and Parliament and the discovery that stopped it in time.

stories.title: Boston Tea Party
stories.description: A factual story of the night Boston protesters destroyed East India Company tea and pushed the imperial crisis toward revolution.

stories.title: Lexington and Concord
stories.description: This factual narrative follows the first day of open fighting in the American Revolution, from Lexington Green to the long road back to Boston.

stories.title: Crossing of the Delaware and Battle of Trenton
stories.description: A factual account of Washington's winter crossing and the surprise victory at Trenton that revived the American cause.

stories.title: Siege of Yorktown
stories.description: This factual story follows the siege that trapped Cornwallis and made clear that the American war for independence could be won.

stories.title: Storming of the Bastille
stories.description: A factual retelling of the day Paris crowds seized the Bastille and turned a political crisis into a revolution in the streets.

stories.title: Reign of Terror
stories.description: This factual narrative explores the period when revolutionary France turned to emergency rule, fear, and execution in the name of saving the republic.

stories.title: Battle of Waterloo
stories.description: A factual account of Napoleon's final gamble and the battle that ended his return to power.

stories.title: Charge of the Light Brigade
stories.description: This factual story follows the Crimean War cavalry charge that became famous for courage, confusion, and disastrous command.

stories.title: Battle of Hampton Roads
stories.description: A factual account of the ironclad clash between USS Monitor and CSS Virginia that changed naval war forever.

stories.title: Sinking of the USS Cairo
stories.description: This factual story follows the loss of a Union ironclad on the Yazoo River and the growing danger of mines in Civil War river warfare.

stories.title: Hunley Attack on USS Housatonic
stories.description: A factual account of the Confederate submarine attack that sank USS Housatonic and showed how underwater war was changing.

stories.title: New York City Draft Riots
stories.description: This factual narrative examines the 1863 riots that exposed class anger, political tension, and racist violence in wartime New York.

stories.title: Great Chicago Fire
stories.description: A factual account of the fire that tore through Chicago in 1871 and forced the city to rebuild after catastrophe.

stories.title: Wounded Knee Massacre
stories.description: This factual story follows the killing at Wounded Knee and the violent end of U.S. conquest policy on the Plains.

stories.title: Sinking of the Titanic
stories.description: A factual narrative of the Titanic disaster, from ice warnings and collision to lifeboats, rescue, and lasting questions about safety.

stories.title: Gallipoli Campaign
stories.description: This factual account follows the failed Allied attempt to force the Dardanelles and the hard campaign that followed on the Gallipoli Peninsula.

stories.title: Russian October Revolution
stories.description: A factual story of the Bolshevik seizure of power in Petrograd and the collapse of the Provisional Government in 1917.

stories.title: Fall of Kabul
stories.description: This factual account follows the collapse of the Afghan government in August 2021 and the desperate rush toward evacuation.
